response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile individual contributor with roughly 15 years of hands-on development experience, genuine developer-tooling interests (test frameworks, CI infrastructure, MCP code generators), and a track record of teaching and community mentorship. However, this is a Level II people-management role requiring at least 2 years of people management and 3 years of formal technical leadership, and the resume shows neither: every position is an IC role, with no evidence of managing direct reports, running one-on-ones, owning team roadmaps, or leading organizational change. The gap on the core requirement is disqualifying for a manager search, though the profile could fit a senior/staff IC opening on the same team.
strengths: Deep, broad engineering background (web, mobile, embedded) with 15+ years of hands-on experience
Authentic developer-tooling and testing focus: test runners, assertion libraries, CI/dev-ops setup, Selenium courses, MCP code generators
Proven mentorship and knowledge-sharing through workshops, conference talks, and code katas
concerns: No people-management experience at all, against a hard requirement of 2+ years
No formal technical-leadership role with roadmap or team-priority ownership demonstrated
Much of the developer-tools work is personal open-source projects rather than sustained professional ownership of build/test infrastructure at scale

key_factors.factor: Hands-on developer tooling and test infrastructure skills
